programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.desc: McGee's girlfriend Delilah makes a breakthrough in a case involving the alleged death of a US navy lieutentant. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An estate agent and two clients discover the body of Lt Com Maya Burris, a naval attache who had been working in Egypt, and her detective husband Nick arrives on the scene to demand answers. Using documents discovered on the victim's boat, Abby links her to Barry Norton, who is suspected of having sold government secrets to a Middle Eastern terrorist group known as the SRC

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs goes into his favourite diner for a cup of coffee and finds himself staring down the barrel of a gun, forcing him to question choices he has made in the past and present. The action then flashes back a few days to when he and the rest of the team members were investigating the death of a petty officer on board USS Gentry, which has been undergoing repairs in dry dock. The 200th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy captain wearing a colourful costume underneath his uniform is shot and killed in a flower shop, and further investigation reveals he was leading a double life as a masked vigilante. Vance brings in Tony's ex-fiancee, journalist Wendy Miller, who claims the victim had been trying to expose a scandal, and as the team pursues the case, a real-estate scam is uncovered. Guest starring Perrey Reeves (Entourage)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy reservist is found dead in his home with marks around his neck, and Gibbs and the team discover he was working on a highly classified case for the department of defence. The circumstances surrounding the man's demise lead the investigators to suspect someone was secretly trying to drive him insane, bringing them into contact with Samantha Ryan, the director of a division specialising in psychological warfare. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. The team discovers the cardiac arrest was caused by someone hacking into a computer monitoring Wiley's pacemaker and increasing his heart rate to 400bpm. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents examine the death of a seaman and find themselves working alongside Barrett. Gibbs makes his displeasure about the circumstances of the case well known, prompting the rest of the investigators to worry that he might be about to leave the NCIS again. Meanwhile, Ziva introduces her new boyfriend to her team-mates. Crime drama, guest starring Enrique Murciano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy lieutenant is found dead in the Pentagon's custodial closet, and surveillance footage indicates she was killed by a co-worker. However, Ziva and the team discover her supposed assailant actually died of a heart attack days before the crime was committed. Meanwhile, suspicions are raised regarding the eyeball left in Tony's drink, and the investigators' attention is fixed on the port-to-port murderer, who appears to still be at large

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony's one-time Baltimore PD partner Danny Price is murdered in a manner that suggests the port-to-port killer has struck again. programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS

Meanwhile, Bishop and her husband invite the team out for a special dinner. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

However, there are one or two disasters Victor does not quite believe, such as rising damp, a plague of mice and a troublesome boxer - not forgetting the dreaded donkeys. Guest starring Joan Sims and Peter Cook

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: During a performance of Hamlet, young cast member Ben Faulkner collapses and dies. Initially the detectives think Ben died of natural causes, until Mike uncovers a complex web of ego-driven dissension among the troupe and follows his nose to the real cause of death

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The owners of a Tuscan inn are sent a severed finger and a photo of their son. Convinced he has been kidnapped by the Mafia, their daughter hires Hetty while she and Robert are on holiday

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The former leader of an Irish Republican group comes forward with information regarding the abduction and subsequent disappearance of an 18-year-old woman in 1983, prompting the team to reopen the case. The reformed dissident claims the rebels used the kidnapping to raise their profile, but his subsequent reinvention as a politician and man of peace leaves Sandra and her colleagues suspicious of his motives. They also investigate whether the arms-manufacturing activities of the victim's father could have made her a target. Crime drama, guest starring Sian Phillips and Annette Crosbie, with Amanda Redman and James Bolam

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony moves in with Deborah, while Gary and Dorothy decide to start a family, but the strain of finally growing up soon takes its toll on the foursome, not least Gary, who becomes overwhelmed by the prospect of fatherhood. Starring Neil Morrissey, Caroline Quentin and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The human race is caught in the middle of an interplanetary war, as two of the Doctor's deadliest enemies - the Daleks and the Cybermen - battle it out on the streets of present-day London. As the Daleks prepare to activate their mysterious secret weapon, the Doctor joins forces with rebels from the Cybermen's own world to get rid of both alien armies

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Martha Jones's life takes a dramatic turn when the hospital she works in is transported to the moon, confronting her with a force of alien stormtroopers called the Judoon, a bloodsucking alien fugitive and a first meeting with the Doctor. David Tennant stars as the time traveller, with Freema Agyeman making her debut as his companion

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor takes Martha to Elizabethan England, where the discovery that William Shakespeare is under the control of sinister witch-like creatures throws the pair into a battle to stop history being changed for ever. Time-travelling adventure, starring David Tennant and Freema Agyeman, with Dean Lennox Kelly as the Bard

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Stephen gets a drenching during a moonlit walk with Jacqui and an abandoned baby opens old wounds for Ruth. Elsewhere, Vic helps Sandra in her hour of need while Peggy is away on her Italian course. Family drama, starring Sarah Lancashire and Tony Haygarth

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma is forced to rearrange her plans for the festive period at the last minute, leaving Hester and William disappointed they will not get to spend their first Christmas with their grandson. However, it remains to be seen who will help them consume all the extra food and drink they have to hand. Christmas special of the comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: James Tennant reports that a local paper has taken a call from a man claiming to have his daughter Amy and an arrest soon follows. June's handling of three youths brought in for vandalism makes an impression on single headmaster Rod Jessop, and Mia gets Mickey Webb to open up about his past relationships. Tony gets involved in police work while moonlighting as a cabbie - to Dan Casper's amusement

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lewis Hardy heroically rescues a man from a burning building, while partner Roger Valentine returns to duty only to suffer a panic attack when he's faced with a crisis. Suzie Sim passes her exam and starts looking for a new post, and Adam Okaro sets his sights on becoming Borough Commander, but is annoyed to find he has tough competition

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Two girls go missing in circumstances that echo the Amy Tennant case and Mickey Webb is horrified to find he knows the fretful parents. Yvonne and Laura find the girls, beaten but alive, and as six-year-old Molly is rushed into intensive care, her older sister Charlotte gives the desperate CID officers information leading to an arrest. Former EastEnders actress Hannah Waterman guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Molly Parker makes a slow recovery, sister Charlotte makes a shocking confession that prompts Mickey Webb to confront their father with barely concealed fury. James Tennant faces further anguish when it becomes clear Amy's disappearance is not related to the Parker case. Terry Perkins considers building bridges with his estranged family, but ex-wife Helen is not easily impressed

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Marsham learns his wife has breast cancer and tries to make amends for neglecting her, shelving a planned move to Scotland and begging Robert and Eleanor to perform a life-saving operation. Medical drama, starring Jemma Redgrave and David Calder

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is flattered by the attentions of charming Dr Hunter, at least on first impressions, but soon changes her opinion when his housekeeper dies suddenly

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The doctor is summoned by farmer's wife Mrs Henderson, who presents him with a puzzling case to solve involving her son, while Dr Cameron moves to his country cottage

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Neil is alarmed that medical tests failed to reveal a patient's illness, and an emergency vaccination programme is hurriedly set up to deal with a suspected outbreak of smallpox

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The introduction of the contraceptive pill is welcomed by Dr Turner, but some of his colleagues are less keen on the idea. Patsy tries to help a bargee who wants to give birth among her own community rather than in a maternity ward. Nurse Crane deals with an emergency during a storm, and a familiar face returns to Nonnatus House - but has been changed by her time away

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is horrified to realise he may be partly to blame as the cause of the recent rise in birth deformities comes to light, and tries to prevent further cases. Sister Evangelina returns to work, but has clearly been affected badly by her stroke, and her health continues to deteriorate. A pregnant bride goes into labour during her wedding reception, while her mother is forced to come to terms with her grandchild being conceived out of wedlock

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Social climber Hyacinth indulges another of her flamboyant whims and decides she and Richard need a new car - but a modest three-door hatchback is not what she had in mind. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to share her unparalleled experience of being the perfect hostess by advertising her talents in the local newspaper. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: It is 1931 and socialite Iris Carr (Tuppence Middleton) is travelling back from a holiday in the Balkans, but faints on the platform of the railway station in the scorching heat. She is comforted by fellow passenger Miss Froy (Selina Cadell), but when Iris falls asleep she awakes to find the woman has disappeared and everyone else on board denies ever having seen her. With only handsome traveller Max Hare (Tom Hughes) for support, Iris maintains that Miss Froy has somehow been abducted - even though it increasingly appears to be a delusion on her part. Mystery, also starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones, Stephanie Cole and Julian Rhind-Tutt. Adapted from Ethel Lina White's novel, The Wheel Spins

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: Alexa investigates a case that runs the gamut of birth, marriage and murder when a young man is found dead in an alley after a fight at his brother's wedding. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The Edinburgh detective investigates the murder of notorious gangster Morris Cafferty's illegitimate son, as the search goes on to find the person who put Sammy in a life-threatening coma. The hunt for the killer is complicated by apparent links to a spate of hate crimes by racist extremists in the city centre. Gritty crime thriller, starring John Hannah and James Cosmo

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When a wealthy banker is found dead at the bottom of her stairs, suspicion immediately falls on her media personality husband, who claims that she fell. Harry conducts the official post-mortem for the police, but finds himself on opposite sides of the case when Nikki performs a second one for the husband's solicitor - and comes up with very different results. Guest starring John Lynch

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Sequel to The White Queen, set in the Middle Ages and picking up as the tumultuous War of the Roses comes to an end, a new generation ascends to the throne in a tale of power, political clashes, family, love and betrayal. As the victor of the Battle of Bosworth, Henry Tudor, is set to ascend the throne and Elizabeth of York and her family, supporters of the former King, find themselves in grave danger. Lizzie must fulfil her promise to marry King Henry VII and bring peace to the nation. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.desc: Elin Fflur chats to singer Aloma Jones, finding out about her career and the sort of person she is at home

programme.title: 04 Wal
programme.desc: Aled Samuel pays a visit to architect Iwan Thomas to inspect his house in Aberaeron. He also looks round an Indian-style bungalow, and makes a journey to Llansteffan to view a seaside home

programme.title: 04 Wal
programme.desc: Aled Samuel visits a host of unusual Welsh houses, including Abergavenny's medieval Penclawdd Court, an interesting family home in Dolgellau, and a house in Cardiff built in a retro style

programme.title: Celwydd Noeth
programme.desc: Quiz show, hosted by Nia Roberts, in which contestants try to win the £10,000 jackpot by picking out the lies among facts and statements in each round

programme.title: Celwydd Noeth
programme.desc: Quiz show, hosted by Nia Roberts, in which friends Llyr and Bethan try to win the £10,000 jackpot by picking out the lies among facts and statements in each round

programme.title: Ty am Ddim
programme.desc: Two women in Anglesey buy a house together, having only known each other for a couple of hours, and have just six months to renew it before selling for a profit

programme.title: Waliau'n Siarad
programme.desc: Aled Hughes and Sara Huws delve into the story of a building that tells of Merthyr Tydfil's proud iron heritage, and of poverty, violence and tragedy

programme.title: Ffermio
programme.desc: Country and farming magazine programme, including features on all aspects of agriculture and rural life

programme.title: Pobol y Cwm
programme.desc: Siw Hughes looks back at memorable storylines featuring Kath in the latest in the series of archive episodes showcasing the soap's most popular characters

programme.title: Pobol y Cwm
programme.desc: The highlights series focuses on the character of Colin, featuring memorable scenes and recollections from the actor who plays him, Jonathan Nefydd

programme.title: Newyddion a Chwaraeon
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Dechrau Canu Dechrau Canmol
programme.desc: Highlights of performances featured throughout the series, including songs by Côr Glanaethwy, Gwyneth Glyn, Elgan Llyr Thomas, Côr Siambr Cymru, and Mary-Jane O'Doherty

programme.title: Lle Bach Mawr
programme.desc: Creative designers Carwyn Lloyd Jones, Mandy Watkins and Gwyn Eiddior accept the challenge of renovating a small space into an amazing area. This week's task is to transform an ordinary garden shed. How will this creative crew get on with converting such a limited space into a remarkable place?

programme.title: Clwb Rygbi
programme.desc: Action from the 10th round of fixtures in the competition involving New Zealand's five Super Rugby teams, featuring Highlanders v Hurricanes and Blues v Crusaders

programme.title: Huw Edwards a'r Cymry Estron
programme.desc: Huw Edwards examines how many of the pillars of modern Wales came from the centre of the English capital, as well as the institutions that serve the expatriate Welsh community in London

programme.title: Prosiect Pum Mil
programme.desc: Emma Walford and Trystan Ellis-Morris are in Kidwelly this week as they work with the local residents to create a sensory garden for the community

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. With Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein return to their investigation of a toxic love triangle, which culminated in the murder of farm owner Frederick Morton

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander visits a masionette over three floors in Crystal Palace, south-east London. Martel Maxwell is in Leeds where a troublesome tree is causing issues at a three bed semi, and Dion Dublin is in Doncaster at a characterful cottage in a bad state of repair

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross visits an unusual chapel in Knaresborough

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Shannon walks in on a couple of surprises at work. Australian drama following the lives of the residents of the inner-city neighbourhood of Arcadia Towers

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of experts from the worlds of history, science and antiques comprising of Geoffrey Munn, Tessa Dunlop, Curtis Dowling, Simon Singh and Anita Anand sets out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a property search in rural Somerset, helping a couple invest their £500,000 in a home that strikes the balance between country charm and practical open planning

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich reveal their favourite water features, including a Japanese-inspired pond complete with teahouse

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary is preparing for the grand reopening of the restaurant, and the sight of him in his best chef whites has Miranda in a spin - he has never looked so handsome. Stevie's advice is simple - she has to tell him how she feels before it's too late. But can the clumsy shopkeeper bring herself to utter those three little words? Or will her mother ruin her attempts as she excitedly stalks her poster boy Raymond Blanc?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenter Richard Hammond, tennis coach Judy Murray and comedians Sean Lock and Trevor Noah join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The school debs is on the horizon, and Conor and Jock are looking forward to making it a great night for the girls. With Principal Walsh intent on getting in their way, the lads are determined to overcome the obstacles and make it a night to remember

programme.title: The Railway Man (2013)
programme.desc: A traumatised former prisoner of war gets married, but continues to suffer nightmares about his experiences. He is unable to talk to his wife about his time in captivity, so she tracks down a fellow hostage to learn the truth - and discovers the cruel Japanese officer he is fixated on is still alive. Fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Nicole Kidman and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: VJ Day 75
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth presents coverage of a special commemoration of the end of the Second World War, broadcast live from the National Memorial Arboretum in Staffordshire. With contributions from Fergal Keane, Dan Snow and veterans of the conflict. The Prince of Wales will lead the two-minute silence at 11.00

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning oversees proceedings in Kingston-upon-Thames, where experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian are helping today's teams spend £300 on three items

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Herculis meeting. Action from the third round of the revised campaign, held at Stade Louis II, where Great Britain's Katarina Johnson-Thompson, was expected to be in action

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session of the final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first eight frames of the match take place

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of comedy, as Ralph Ineson, John Thomson, Janine Duvitski, Tony Maudsley, Melvyn Hayes, Stuart McGugan, Vicki Pepperdine and Rosie Cavaliero take part

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, contestants include twins from Hull, a professional face painter from Bristol and a prison officer from Preston

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dylan is torn between wanting to be honest with Faith about Lev cheating on her, and knowing that the truth would ruin her life at an already trying time. He finally decides to tell her the heartbreaking truth, and she goes to confront Lev. As her bump continues to grow, Fenisha wonders how much longer she can keep her pregnancy a secret. Meanwhile, Jacob treats Multiple Sclerosis sufferer Felix, but quickly becomes concerned when it is revealed that he was diagnosed abroad, and thinks that there may be another reason behind his symptoms

programme.title: VJ Day 75
programme.desc: A spectacular event pre-recorded on Horse Guards Parade, telling the story of the campaign in the Far East that helped bring the Second World War to an end. Key moments of the conflict as it developed will be reflected through testimonies, readings and musical performances, and will also feature current serving personnel. The Duke of Cambridge will also give an address paying tribute to the veterans and the wartime generation, as the nation honours, thanks and remembers all those who sacrificed so much during the war. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel

programme.title: Young Frankenstein (1974)
programme.desc: Frederick Frankenstein rejects his family heritage as a mad scientist, but when he finds a book revealing how his grandfather created a monster, he cannot resist building one of his own. Mel Brooks' spoof of James Whale's classic 1930s horror films, starring Gene Wilder, Marty Feldman, Madeline Kahn, Teri Garr, Peter Boyle and Gene Hackman

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a terraced house in Derby, a commercial property near Newcastle-under-Lyme and a bungalow in the Welsh Valleys

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Nick Hall help two teams look for items at Nottingham racecourse, while presenter Charlie tests the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Trentham Gardens in Staffordshire, discovering how the estate has been a place of Christian worship since the 7th century due to an ancient pilgrimage route

programme.title: Jane Eyre (2011)
programme.desc: A girl raised in misery by her cruel aunt grows up to become a governess, and accepts work at the estate of a mysterious landowner. Despite being disturbed by strange goings-on in the house, she falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover he is concealing a terrible secret. Period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Michael Fassbender, Jamie Bell and Judi Dench

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps two expats who want to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is helped by Norman Wilkinson, the Bristol Upholstery Collective and Guy Trench to transform fencing, a pair of demi-johns, a nursing chair and a fire-damaged table

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes view of the extraordinary idea of deploying the Spy Creatures, showing how the concept evolved and became the inspiration for the animatronic animals of the series. This documentary reveals the painstaking work behind building the lifelike models, from first concept until they become 'alive' for the first time. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, one of the first stately homes in Britain to be converted into a hospital and convalescent home during the First World War. Items brought in to be valued include a diamond and ruby pendant, a rare snuff container, and competing pieces of Daum glassware

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: While Haresh is preoccupied with finding himself a superior job, competition for Lata's hand grows when she returns to Brahmpur and Kabir Durrani re-enters her life. Further complicating matters of the heart, she receives a gift with a romantic message hidden within it. Maan's long-awaited reunion with Saeeda Bai is short-lived when he inadvertently offends her, and he finds himself at the centre of religious tensions and the threat of violence when the festivals of Moharram and Dussehra coincide

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: The barristers return to their investigation of a curious case, a poultry farmer who buried the body of his fiancé under a chicken run, but claimed to be innocent of her murder

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: David and Harry Rich remember their favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including an awkwardly sloped plot given two tiers and a tiny garden turned into a wildlife paradise

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: There is a sombre mood at the manor when Shakespeare fails to return, seeming to confirm concerns that he died defending Flower's pups in the Lazuli attack. Meanwhile, Youssarian hopes to become the dominant male of his clan

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: An early dusting of snow signals the end of autumn, a time when the forests are a blaze of red and gold and the animals prepare for the coming cold. The elk face hungry wolves as they move down from the mountains to feed in the valleys, while beavers rush to defend their habitat against the destructive ice, repairing dams and stocking underwater larders before the waters freeze over

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Ronnie O'Sullivan v Mark Selby. Semi-final coverage continues at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, as the penultimate session is played in the second semi-final in the bottom half of the draw

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Kyren Wilson v Anthony McGill. Coverage of the concluding session of the opening semi-final in Sheffield, where the first player to reach 17 frames will progress to the final, to be played over the next two days

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers take Flower's new pups on their first foraging trip and are caught out in the open by another group of war-dancing meerkats. Meanwhile, two males who want to mate with Lazuli females join forces with Yossarian

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Ronnie O'Sullivan v Mark Selby. Further coverage from Sheffield, where the second semi-final is set to reach a conclusion, and the line-up for the final of the showpiece tournament decided after 15 days of play. Judd Trump held a 14-10 lead heading into the final session of his semi-final against Gary Wilson last year, and the Juddernaut accelerated to victory and a place in the final against John Higgins by winning three of the next four frames

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Featuring a man in Margate who has come up with a unique way of using a shed roof to grow vegetables, and inspirational gardener Sue Kent in Swansea, who shows how she has designed her plot to enable her to garden successfully despite her upper limb difference. Last year, Joe Swift went to Devon to visit a garden where the planting combinations have been inspired by world travel and in Hampshire a sea captain explains how he has created a low maintenance garden using foliage and structural plants

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Actor Richard E Grant looks back at the year when he starred in cult classic Withnail & I, and Rick Astley, Terence Trent D'Arby, Run DMC and the Beastie Boys all hit the charts. TV screens saw the arrival of US drama Thirtysomething, while Damon and Debbie eloped in Brookside

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the second day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: The Edge (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about England's Test cricket team between 2009 and 2013, the first and only English side to ever reach the top of the world rankings. Featuring contributions from Andrew Strauss, Kevin Pietersen, Alastair Cook, James Anderson, Stuart Broad, Monty Panesar, Graeme Swann, Jonathan Trott, Matt Prior, Ian Bell, Paul Collingwood, Steven Finn, Tim Bresnan, David Saker and Andy Flower

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Tom Heap investigates the impact of the lockdown due to the coronavirus pandemic on Britain's bfood manufacturing sector, and looks at the transformation in the way people shop, cook and consume food

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: Rupert Murdoch's involvement in the 2016 US elections and the Brexit referendum, which saw the media mogul rising in prominence after a series of scandals and setbacks, from the death of his mother and the collapse of his second marriage to the loss of several important political allies. The programme examines the resolution of the question of Murdoch's successor and a final multi-billion dollar deal that surprises everyone

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Four wedding planners are determined to get Mum and Dad muddy on their big day

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine the rocks that like to take a stroll in the desert, a pebble hot enough to burn a hole in a person's pocket and an exploding boulder

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends take part in a cheese race in Caerphilly

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield create a wheelchair-accessible old-fashioned cinema den full of Hollywood glamour for 11-year-old movie buff Lola and her mother

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to the rainforest in Eduador in search of some of the deadliest critters.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: A real-life King Kong shows Steve who is boss, and a scaly thief is caught red-handed.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching-He Huang and Glynn Purnell, and Marian Keyes via video link. Including clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Nigella Lawson

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on street food, making falafael with beetroot houmous and Greek salad. They are joined by street food chef Rachna Dheer, from the Babu Bombay Street Kitchen in Glasgow, and Call the Midwife actress Laura Main, who selects a Scottish gin to accompany the food

programme.title: The Good Cook
programme.desc: Simon Hopkinson demonstrates how to prepare restaurant-quality food at home, preparing dishes including potato soup with soured cream and chives, fried lamb slice with mayonnaise, sweet and sour calf's liver and onions, and a ginger chocolate pot

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Former Strictly Come Dancing judge Len Goodman takes ex-footballer and TV presenter Dion Dublin on a journey to Great Yarmouth to recreate a seaside holiday from 1979. The pair travel in a 1970s Morris Marina Coupe, get competitive in a football match, play a game of air hockey in the arcade, and compete in Segway racing

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nathaniel Baker believes his family's future lies in Australia, but wife Gemma sees things very differently, so they check out properties, work opportunities and life in Melbourne

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At the recycling centre in Woking, EJ Osborne unearths three hidden gems with profit-making potential. To transform his trash, EJ calls on robot-maker Mark Haig to find a new use for an unwanted tape player, while designer maker Rupert Blanchard has the tall order of making half a chest of drawers whole again. A draughting chair is also given the upcycling treatment by EJ

programme.title: Big Cats
programme.desc: The final documentary focuses on the scientists who are studying wild cats in more detail than ever before - and the ground-breaking discoveries they are making. Professor Alan Wilson has been researching the cheetah, using hi-tech collars to rewrite what is understood about the fastest animal on land - revealing that its speed is not its greatest weapon. There is also a look at cat conservation, with stories including that of the Iberian lynx. Just five years ago this was considered the rarest cat on the planet. Now, thanks to a captive breeding programme, numbers are increasing in the wild

programme.title: Love & Friendship (2016)
programme.desc: Lady Susan Vernon arrives at her brother-in-law's residence, Churchill, with a matchmaking plan for her daughter, having left a trail of confusion behind her at her last residence, Manwaring. While young Reginald de Courcey is charmed by her, some of her family are deeply suspicious of her motives. But few people are equal to her schemes. Period comedy based on Jane Austen's novella Lady Susan, starring Kate Beckinsale, Chloe Sevigny and Stepheny Fry

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the opening session of the final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first eight frames of the match take place

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: This time, Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlin's Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schöneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movement's first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the second session of the final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where another nine frames take place. Judd Trump was in commanding form over John Higgins in last year's corresponding session, winning all but one of the frames to race into a 12-5 overall lead, leaving him in need of just six frames the following day to claim his maiden world title. Higgins was unable to overturn that deficit and will not get another chance to go one better this time around as he was eliminated by Kurt Maflin in the second round. However, he did have the consolation of scoring the first maximum 147 break in the competition since 2012 in the defeat to Maflin, collecting a bonus of £40,000

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the third day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: The King's Choice (2016)
programme.desc: With the German army advancing on Norway, King Haakon VII faces a decision that will change his country forever. Historical drama, starring Jesper Christensen, Anders Baasmo Christiansen and Karl Markovics. In Norwegian, Danish and German

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: MasterChef's John Torode reaches the letter W in his alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive, highlighting Rick Stein's potato and watercress soup, and the Hairy Bikers' white chocolate cheesecake

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is in the Cotswolds at the Broadway Tower folly to look back at unusual events that have featured on the programme over the years. Including the time Anita Rani met a farmer growing a crop of chairs, and when Margherita Taylor made microscopic art from nature

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Featuring a man in Margate who has come up with a unique way of using a shed roof to grow vegetables, and inspirational gardener Sue Kent in Swansea, who shows how she has designed her plot to enable her to garden successfully despite her upper limb difference. Last year, Joe Swift went to Devon to visit a garden where the planting combinations have been inspired by world travel and in Hampshire a sea captain explains how he has created a low maintenance garden using foliage and structural plants

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter checks up on her chilies and peppers in her countryside garden as well as the cucumbers in her polytunnel, and Kirsty Wilson tries out an easy-to-use propagator to make houseplants for free. Sophie McKilligan returns to her Aberdeen allotment after a break to visit family, and finds the rest of the community have kindly looked after her patch while she was away

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner discusses culinary memories with Anneka Rice, revisiting her childhood in Surrey, and creates two dishes that take her back to that time

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste explore the benefits of nature, with a trip to the woods and a day spent working on a farm

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The duo arrive in Italy, where they abandon their hogs for the gondolas of Venice, and bake a classic focaccia by the side of a canal. They later visit Si King's sister, who lives in Tuscany, and savour a pasta pie in the back streets of Ferrara. There then follows a dash for Turin to help celebrate the country's 150th birthday, a visit to a hazelnut farm to learn the secrets of a certain chocolate spread and the chance to re-create big-screen movie The Italian Job on the rooftop of the old Fiat factory

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage from the third session of the final, as the players face a potential eight frames in the ranking tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield. John Higgins and Judd Trump won four frames apiece in this session last year, which was of greater benefit to Trump, as it allowed him to go into the final session with a 16-9 lead, thus needing just two more frames to claim victory

programme.title: Sir Chris Hoy's Secret World of Japanese Bicycle Racing
programme.desc: The Olympic great explores the keirin, one of the most extreme and exciting events in track cycling, examining its origin and popularity in Japan. He makes a return to the country after being invited there for a keirin school in 2005, which is considered a great honour for international cyclists

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness look back at highlights of the last two series of the motoring show. Featuring Freddie trying to bungee-jump a car off a dam, Paddy proving an old hearse can be the ultimate family car, and Chris remembering the iconic Lotus 79 race car

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: All the action from the concluding session of this year's final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first player to reach 18 frames is crowned champion. Judd Trump needed just two frames to wrap up an impressive 18-9 victory in this session last year, recording breaks of 94 and 63 to comfortably overcome the four-time champion and claim his maiden title. 'The Juddernaut' has won six ranking competitions since to cement his status at the top of the rankings and put him among the pre-tournament favourites to prevail once again. However, former champions including Mark Selby, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Neil Robertson and Mark Williams also entered with high hopes and all made it past the first round making a winner very difficult to call

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the fourth day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The police visit Niki in search of information about her fugitive husband DL Hawkins, while Matt puts his mind-reading skills to good use in a bid to improve his marriage. Hiro and Ando find themselves out of their depth in Las Vegas, and Peter receives a life-changing message from a surprising source

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Niki has a surprise visitor and later confronts her mirror image, while Hiro and Ando find their journey to New York delayed when they land themselves in trouble in Las Vegas. Elsewhere, Claire meets her biological parents in an attempt to shed light on her powers, and Hiro sends a vital message to Isaac

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: When Iraq's president Saddam Hussein was captured by coalition forces in December 2003, politicians in the White House believed this would be the turning point they needed to bring democracy to Iraq. This edition looks at how Saddam's removal led to a new chapter of conflict as sectarian differences flared up to plunge Iraq into a civil war. Senior CIA analyst John Nixon was the first person to question Saddam, and the dictator's answers during the five-week interrogation left the American both surprised and horrified

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Guy finds Ric lying unconscious in theatre, and decides to seize the opportunity he is presented with. It falls to Louis and Donna to uncover his intentions and stop him before it is too late. Sacha is encouraged by Fletch to set things right with Essie, only to discover she has big plans for the future. Nicky is hurt by Cameron's odd behaviour towards her, and receives an unexpected offer

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the second day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A red-hot selection of summer sounds, featuring songs by John Travolta and Olivia Newton-John, the Undertones, Shaggy, Bananarama, Bobby Goldsboro, Bay City Rollers, Fun Boy Three, the Style Council and Don Henley. Other archive sizzlers include DJ Jazzy Jeff and the Fresh Prince, the Sundays, Sabrina, Chris Rea, the Barracudas, Zoe, Martha and the Muffins, Bryan Adams, Girls Aloud and ELO

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage from the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield, as the second semi-final reaches a conclusion, with the players needing to reach 17 frames to secure their place in the final

programme.title: Joan Armatrading: Me Myself I
programme.desc: Known for her reclusiveness and barriers, the singer has for the first time granted access to her life and music. In this film she recounts her story from Caribbean emigre to being one of the most revered songwriters of her generation. The film covers Joan's childhood in Birmingham, her involvement in the musical Hair in 1968, her musical partnership with songwriter Pam Nestor and the relationship later forged with rock producer Glyn Johns. Plus, how she diversified her style in the 1980s, experimenting with synths and finding a new direction to create music without a producer

programme.title: Queens of Soul
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage to celebrate the critically-acclaimed female singers whose voices have come to define the soul genre, including Aretha Franklin, Roberta Flack, Gladys Knight, Randy Crawford, Angie Stone, Mary J Blige and Beyonce

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: A performance by the Brit Award-winning artist at the BBC Radio Theatre in London, recorded last week and featuring songs from Our Version of Events and Long Live the Angels

programme.title: The Defiant Ones
programme.desc: Dr Dre talks about his time with Snoop Dogg and recent signee Tupac Shakur, who all became embroiled in a violent feud with East Coast rap rivals, notably Sean 'Puffy' Combs and his Bad Boy Records. This drove Dre to seek out another new beginning and establish a subsidiary of Interscope called Aftermath Entertainment. Dre discusses enduring a string of disappointing solo releases, but Jimmy stood by him as others argued he should be dropped. However, Dre's fortunes turned when Jimmy turned him on to the music of unknown white rapper Eminem

programme.title: Joan Armatrading: Me Myself I

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: With their cute faces and extraordinary head-turning ability, owls are one of the nation's best-loved birds, but it's rare to catch more than a glimpse of one in the wild. With the help of leading scientists, this programme examines how the birds hunt in the dark and why they are able to fly so quietly, getting an insight into their world through the eyes of two barn owl chicks. Paul McGann narrates

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Detectives from Denmark and Sweden are forced to work together when a body is found on a bridge between the two countries. However, further investigation reveals the corpse has a gruesome secret - and investigators Saga Noren and Martin Rohde realise the criminal they are chasing will stop at nothing to get his message across. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The killer selects sharp-witted journalist Daniel Ferbe to deliver a message to the rest of the world, claiming the murder on the bridge was committed to draw attention to social injustice. As Saga and Martin analyse the villain's motive, they realise they are dealing with someone who believes he is serving the greater good. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The killer's next brutal act results in the deaths of several homeless people in Copenhagen, supposedly to highlight how easily people can fall through the cracks in Danish society. He then kidnaps another rough sleeper - and demands a sizeable ransom from four of the city's landlords to spare his life. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: As the murderer's deadline draws closer, it appears the fate of the homeless man he has kidnapped lies in the hands of the four landlords. However, Saga and Martin devise an alternative plan - and hope to catch their target off guard by doing something he does not expect. In Danish and Swedish

programme.desc: A collection of performances from the BBC archives celebrating the sights and sounds of London. programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by Stephen Fry for one of his favourite concerts, as Daniel Barenboim conducts his West-Eastern Divan Orchestra in Liszt's Piano Concerto No 1, performed in 2016 by his childhood friend, pianist Martha Argerich, along with epic orchestral performances from three different operas by Richard Wagner

programme.title: Albion
programme.desc: Mike Bartlett's tragi-comic drama about national identity, family, passion and the disappointment of personal dreams, directed for the stage by Rupert Goold. Filmed at London's Almeida Theatre, the play is set in the garden of an English country house, which has been bought by successful businesswoman Audrey Walters (Victoria Hamilton), who intends to restore the ruined garden to its former glory, and to create a memorial to the son she recently lost in a foreign war

programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. Can Joe fully recover from his surgery and complete the training that will allow him to have a new best friend - his very own hearing dog?

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: All the animals teach Jackal a lesson by telling him the moon is made of honey

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy jets off in his safari-mobile to Australia to learn how weaver ants build nests

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly decides that Alice and James ought to get married. She arranges for them to go on a date at her own homemade cinema, but then the projector breaks down

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Spring arrives, but Katie Morag and Grannie Island become worried when Alecina falls ill after lambing

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The youngster and her feathered friend take part in a treasure hunt when there is a bread festival in the park

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The children have to make pictures of their houses for a school project, but Lotta panics when she forgets how to draw

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: It's a very cold day on the Clangers' Planet. The Iron Chicken has come down to the living cave to get warm. But when a Sky Moo wants to do the same, she finds the doors are frozen shut and she can't get in. So Granny has to come to the rescue with a clever plan to keep her and the other Sky Moos warm

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos hear Igglepiggle's musical stepping stones and Upsy Daisy's singing

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: American country singer Dolly Parton reads Dog Loves Books, by Louise Yates

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. With Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein return to their investigation of a toxic love triangle, which culminated in the murder of farm owner Frederick Morton

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander visits a masionette over three floors in Crystal Palace, south-east London. Martel Maxwell is in Leeds where a troublesome tree is causing issues at a three bed semi, and Dion Dublin is in Doncaster at a characterful cottage in a bad state of repair

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross visits an unusual chapel in Knaresborough

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Shannon walks in on a couple of surprises at work. Australian drama following the lives of the residents of the inner-city neighbourhood of Arcadia Towers

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of experts from the worlds of history, science and antiques comprising of Geoffrey Munn, Tessa Dunlop, Curtis Dowling, Simon Singh and Anita Anand sets out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a property search in rural Somerset, helping a couple invest their £500,000 in a home that strikes the balance between country charm and practical open planning

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich reveal their favourite water features, including a Japanese-inspired pond complete with teahouse

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary is preparing for the grand reopening of the restaurant, and the sight of him in his best chef whites has Miranda in a spin - he has never looked so handsome. Stevie's advice is simple - she has to tell him how she feels before it's too late. But can the clumsy shopkeeper bring herself to utter those three little words? Or will her mother ruin her attempts as she excitedly stalks her poster boy Raymond Blanc?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh telly. This time he's on a quest to discover the truth about politics and protest in Wales. It's a story of deadly asteroids, MPs on Segways, and a brand new party for the undecided, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian has to cope with intense pressure as he joins a fire brigade unit responding to emergency calls

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The school debs is on the horizon, and Conor and Jock are looking forward to making it a great night for the girls. With Principal Walsh intent on getting in their way, the lads are determined to overcome the obstacles and make it a night to remember

programme.title: The Railway Man (2013)
programme.desc: A traumatised former prisoner of war gets married, but continues to suffer nightmares about his experiences. He is unable to talk to his wife about his time in captivity, so she tracks down a fellow hostage to learn the truth - and discovers the cruel Japanese officer he is fixated on is still alive. Fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Nicole Kidman and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: VJ Day 75
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth presents coverage of a special commemoration of the end of the Second World War, broadcast live from the National Memorial Arboretum in Staffordshire. With contributions from Fergal Keane, Dan Snow and veterans of the conflict. The Prince of Wales will lead the two-minute silence at 11.00

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning oversees proceedings in Kingston-upon-Thames, where experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian are helping today's teams spend £300 on three items

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Herculis meeting. Action from the third round of the revised campaign, held at Stade Louis II, where Great Britain's Katarina Johnson-Thompson, was expected to be in action

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session of the final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first eight frames of the match take place

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of comedy, as Ralph Ineson, John Thomson, Janine Duvitski, Tony Maudsley, Melvyn Hayes, Stuart McGugan, Vicki Pepperdine and Rosie Cavaliero take part

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, contestants include twins from Hull, a professional face painter from Bristol and a prison officer from Preston

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dylan is torn between wanting to be honest with Faith about Lev cheating on her, and knowing that the truth would ruin her life at an already trying time. He finally decides to tell her the heartbreaking truth, and she goes to confront Lev. As her bump continues to grow, Fenisha wonders how much longer she can keep her pregnancy a secret. Meanwhile, Jacob treats Multiple Sclerosis sufferer Felix, but quickly becomes concerned when it is revealed that he was diagnosed abroad, and thinks that there may be another reason behind his symptoms

programme.title: VJ Day 75
programme.desc: A spectacular event pre-recorded on Horse Guards Parade, telling the story of the campaign in the Far East that helped bring the Second World War to an end. Key moments of the conflict as it developed will be reflected through testimonies, readings and musical performances, and will also feature current serving personnel. The Duke of Cambridge will also give an address paying tribute to the veterans and the wartime generation, as the nation honours, thanks and remembers all those who sacrificed so much during the war. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel

programme.title: Young Frankenstein (1974)
programme.desc: Frederick Frankenstein rejects his family heritage as a mad scientist, but when he finds a book revealing how his grandfather created a monster, he cannot resist building one of his own. Mel Brooks' spoof of James Whale's classic 1930s horror films, starring Gene Wilder, Marty Feldman, Madeline Kahn, Teri Garr, Peter Boyle and Gene Hackman

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a terraced house in Derby, a commercial property near Newcastle-under-Lyme and a bungalow in the Welsh Valleys

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Nick Hall help two teams look for items at Nottingham racecourse, while presenter Charlie tests the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Trentham Gardens in Staffordshire, discovering how the estate has been a place of Christian worship since the 7th century due to an ancient pilgrimage route

programme.title: Scrum V Classics
programme.desc: Scotland v Wales. A look back at the memorable 1997 Five Nations Championship encounter at Murrayfield

programme.title: Jane Eyre (2011)
programme.desc: A girl raised in misery by her cruel aunt grows up to become a governess, and accepts work at the estate of a mysterious landowner. Despite being disturbed by strange goings-on in the house, she falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover he is concealing a terrible secret. Period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Michael Fassbender, Jamie Bell and Judi Dench

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the Cambrian mountains, heading for the Elan Valley in search of cuckoos. As he soon discovers they are easy to hear but no so easy to see. He also finds red squirrels that are genetically unique to the Cambrians in a remote conifer plantation, and looks for golden plover, one of the rarest breeding birds in Wales, on the hills surrounding Plynlimon

programme.title: The Story of Wales
programme.desc: Huw Edwards focuses on the Industrial Revolution as he continues his exploration of Welsh history. He assesses the impact of the copper trade on the country's landscape, and the importance of iron to Merthyr Tydfil. There is also a look at how the changes sparked social turmoil, riots and uprisings, and led to the first calls for democracy and workers' rights

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, one of the first stately homes in Britain to be converted into a hospital and convalescent home during the First World War. Items brought in to be valued include a diamond and ruby pendant, a rare snuff container, and competing pieces of Daum glassware

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: While Haresh is preoccupied with finding himself a superior job, competition for Lata's hand grows when she returns to Brahmpur and Kabir Durrani re-enters her life. Further complicating matters of the heart, she receives a gift with a romantic message hidden within it. Maan's long-awaited reunion with Saeeda Bai is short-lived when he inadvertently offends her, and he finds himself at the centre of religious tensions and the threat of violence when the festivals of Moharram and Dussehra coincide

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is helped by Norman Wilkinson, the Bristol Upholstery Collective and Guy Trench to transform fencing, a pair of demi-johns, a nursing chair and a fire-damaged table

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes view of the extraordinary idea of deploying the Spy Creatures, showing how the concept evolved and became the inspiration for the animatronic animals of the series.
