programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary is preparing for the grand reopening of the restaurant, and the sight of him in his best chef whites has Miranda in a spin - he has never looked so handsome. Stevie's advice is simple - she has to tell him how she feels before it's too late. But can the clumsy shopkeeper bring herself to utter those three little words? Or will her mother ruin her attempts as she excitedly stalks her poster boy Raymond Blanc?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: The tiny village of Clonmany in Donegal hosts the longest running country music festival in Ireland, and this year it's preparing for its 50th anniversary. With a night of nostalgia focusing on the veteran stars, Philomena Begley and TR Dallas take centre stage in this trip down memory lane

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The school debs is on the horizon, and Conor and Jock are looking forward to making it a great night for the girls. With Principal Walsh intent on getting in their way, the lads are determined to overcome the obstacles and make it a night to remember

programme.title: The Railway Man (2013)
programme.desc: A traumatised former prisoner of war gets married, but continues to suffer nightmares about his experiences. He is unable to talk to his wife about his time in captivity, so she tracks down a fellow hostage to learn the truth - and discovers the cruel Japanese officer he is fixated on is still alive. Fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Nicole Kidman and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: VJ Day 75
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth presents coverage of a special commemoration of the end of the Second World War, broadcast live from the National Memorial Arboretum in Staffordshire. With contributions from Fergal Keane, Dan Snow and veterans of the conflict. The Prince of Wales will lead the two-minute silence at 11.00

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning oversees proceedings in Kingston-upon-Thames, where experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian are helping today's teams spend £300 on three items

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Herculis meeting. Action from the third round of the revised campaign, held at Stade Louis II, where Great Britain's Katarina Johnson-Thompson, was expected to be in action

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session of the final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first eight frames of the match take place

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of comedy, as Ralph Ineson, John Thomson, Janine Duvitski, Tony Maudsley, Melvyn Hayes, Stuart McGugan, Vicki Pepperdine and Rosie Cavaliero take part

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, contestants include twins from Hull, a professional face painter from Bristol and a prison officer from Preston

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dylan is torn between wanting to be honest with Faith about Lev cheating on her, and knowing that the truth would ruin her life at an already trying time. He finally decides to tell her the heartbreaking truth, and she goes to confront Lev. As her bump continues to grow, Fenisha wonders how much longer she can keep her pregnancy a secret. Meanwhile, Jacob treats Multiple Sclerosis sufferer Felix, but quickly becomes concerned when it is revealed that he was diagnosed abroad, and thinks that there may be another reason behind his symptoms

programme.title: VJ Day 75
programme.desc: A spectacular event pre-recorded on Horse Guards Parade, telling the story of the campaign in the Far East that helped bring the Second World War to an end. Key moments of the conflict as it developed will be reflected through testimonies, readings and musical performances, and will also feature current serving personnel. The Duke of Cambridge will also give an address paying tribute to the veterans and the wartime generation, as the nation honours, thanks and remembers all those who sacrificed so much during the war. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel

programme.title: Young Frankenstein (1974)
programme.desc: Frederick Frankenstein rejects his family heritage as a mad scientist, but when he finds a book revealing how his grandfather created a monster, he cannot resist building one of his own. Mel Brooks' spoof of James Whale's classic 1930s horror films, starring Gene Wilder, Marty Feldman, Madeline Kahn, Teri Garr, Peter Boyle and Gene Hackman

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a terraced house in Derby, a commercial property near Newcastle-under-Lyme and a bungalow in the Welsh Valleys

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Nick Hall help two teams look for items at Nottingham racecourse, while presenter Charlie tests the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Trentham Gardens in Staffordshire, discovering how the estate has been a place of Christian worship since the 7th century due to an ancient pilgrimage route

programme.title: Jane Eyre (2011)
programme.desc: A girl raised in misery by her cruel aunt grows up to become a governess, and accepts work at the estate of a mysterious landowner. Despite being disturbed by strange goings-on in the house, she falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover he is concealing a terrible secret. Period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Michael Fassbender, Jamie Bell and Judi Dench

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps two expats who want to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is helped by Norman Wilkinson, the Bristol Upholstery Collective and Guy Trench to transform fencing, a pair of demi-johns, a nursing chair and a fire-damaged table

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes view of the extraordinary idea of deploying the Spy Creatures, showing how the concept evolved and became the inspiration for the animatronic animals of the series. This documentary reveals the painstaking work behind building the lifelike models, from first concept until they become 'alive' for the first time. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, one of the first stately homes in Britain to be converted into a hospital and convalescent home during the First World War. Items brought in to be valued include a diamond and ruby pendant, a rare snuff container, and competing pieces of Daum glassware

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: While Haresh is preoccupied with finding himself a superior job, competition for Lata's hand grows when she returns to Brahmpur and Kabir Durrani re-enters her life. Further complicating matters of the heart, she receives a gift with a romantic message hidden within it. Maan's long-awaited reunion with Saeeda Bai is short-lived when he inadvertently offends her, and he finds himself at the centre of religious tensions and the threat of violence when the festivals of Moharram and Dussehra coincide

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: Coverage of the testimony by the brother of George Floyd to the House of Representatives hearing on police violence, alongside civil rights leaders and a police chief, from Thursday 11 June. First shown on BBC Parliament on Sunday 14 June

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Is the lockdown turning back the clock on women's lives? With women appearing to bear the brunt of the economic fallout of the pandemic, and the UN describing the worldwide increase in domestic abuse as a 'shadow pandemic' alongside Covid-19, many are asking if this is the biggest ever leap backward for women. And yet female leaders at the helm of various nations have been commended for managing the crisis better than their male counterparts - does that reflect that where women are elected as leaders there is a greater presence of women in positions of power across the board?

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. Our evermore interconnected and technologically advanced societies are now in lockdown and we are fearful for our health and economic futures thanks to an invisible virus. Stephen Sackur speaks to the Israeli historian and best-selling author Yuval Noah Harari. What 21st century lesson can we draw from the spread of Covid-19? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Tuesday 28 April.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of a statement by Health Minister Nadine Dorries in response to the publication of the Cumberledge Review into medical device and medicine safety. programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Some are predicting that despite the easing of the lockdown, the arts will be devastated by the financial impact of the pandemic. What will be the effect on the performing arts and on our culture after months in which the cinemas, theatres, concert halls and galleries have been shut down - with huge uncertainty about how and when they will reopen? How will this seismic shock change our culture? Will people start to consume culture increasingly in the privacy of their own homes? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Saturday 4 July.

programme.title: 'San Fhuil (It's in the Blood)
programme.desc: A personal portrait of a South Uist crofting township, where the Greylag geese outnumber people by three to one. This film celebrates a way of life which has positively shaped this dynamic landscape and explores the challenges that the crofters face

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: Seven years in the planning, two years in its construction, the Harris Distillery is in its first year of existence. This first of three episodes follows the lead up to and first working year at the Harris Distillery. After some delays in construction and the inevitable technical glitches, their Harris Gin goes straight into production. Hot on its heels is the distillery's primary function, and the team works up to distilling its first Hearach whisky. The distillery staff have all been recruited locally and are collectively known as the 'Tarbert Ten'. They share a commonality in having never been involved in the distillery business, but are keen to prove that they are more than able to create a historic happening on the Isle of Harris

programme.title: Bonn Còmhraidh

programme.desc: At Tingwall Airport in Shetland, Jim Gray performs the daily runway inspection, but knows that unless the fog clears there will be no flying. Nick McLeod fulfills a childhood dream by building his own Eurofox plane

programme.desc: Tha Bing ‘s a charaidean a cluich geama spòrsail ‘s iad a' leigeil orra gur e rannsachairean-nàdar a th' annta ‘s iad a' lorg rudan le dathan sònraichte. Ge-tà, chan eil Bing a' cumail sùil ro gheur air na tha e a' togail…! Bing and his friends are playing a really fun game where they pretend to be nature explorers as they look for things in the garden that match their colour wheel. programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Tthe challenges of winter on the croft as Sweeny contends with 2018's infamous Beast from the East and some really cold weather. One of these new ideas leads him to a new hobby - knitting - as he heads to the Isle of Skye to get creative with his wool

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Vet Romain Pizzi is at Five Sisters Zoo checking out Billy the coati, who has been suffering from stiffness, and Lewy the ruffed lemur. X-rays reveal good news and bad news

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: The community gathers for Anna's first birthday party. Two unexpected guests arrive - one welcomed with open arms and the other uninvited. Claire now believes she's cracked the case

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Màiri returns from prison to Camus alone, which stirs some strong emotions within the community. Cailean is furious she's back, certain that Màiri and Tormod set him up, but it causes nothing but strife for heavily pregnant Sarah Jane

programme.title: Sgeulachd Deacon Brodie
programme.desc: John Morrison explores the life of Edinburgh-born William Brodie, whose criminal dealings and double identity is believed to have inspired Robert Louis Stevenson's character Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. The presenter investigates why the ambiguous man who was hanged for his affairs in 1889 is still regarded with affection in the Scottish capital, with the help of expert opinion and drama reconstruction

programme.title: Eileanan na Banrighe/Islands of the Queen
programme.desc: Celebrating the people who live and work on some of the beautiful islands of the Commonwealth

programme.title: Eadar Chluich
programme.desc: Iain Glen stars in a short drama adaptation based on Finlay MacNeill's song The Tears Welled

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is joined by antiques dealers Tony Geering, James Layte, Corrie Jeffrey and Alison Chapman in Scarborough, North Yorkshire, to help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. Tony blows someone else's trumpet, James gets danced out of his dosh, Alison drops thousands on a claret jug, while Corrie gets her hand on a sparkling solitaire ring

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quizzy McQuizface, a team of pub quizzers from Scotland, answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is no going back for Dawn, Nate suggests he skip work to support Tracy, and Harriet feels more guilty than ever

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fears that Sarah has shared his darkest secrets, Maria asks David to witness her wedding instead of a sick Audrey, and a spiteful Geoff tells Sally that Tim will always choose him over her

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: American Gangster (2007)
programme.desc: The story of 1970s drug kingpin Frank Lucas, whose innovative methods of smuggling heroin into the US made him one of the leading figures in the New York underworld. A detective is assigned to bring Lucas's empire down, but his investigation is undermined by police corruption. Ridley Scott's fact-based crime drama, starring Denzel Washington, Russell Crowe, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Josh Brolin

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay presents the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is babysitting the Neighbours' toddler. The baby flees and kidnaps Fizz mistaking him for a plush toy.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has invented a machine which stops toast from landing butter-side down. Magoo sees some floating toast and believes a ghost is haunting their house.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is on a beach and that sailors are emptying their oil tanker right into the ocean.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A strange malfunction occurs on a high-speed train in Japan, leaving it out of control and unable to stop. Brains comes up with a plan to fix the problem before a disastrous crash occurs - the only problem is that he will have to board the speeding engine himself to pull it off. Scott manages to get the genius on board, where they find the problem is more complicated than they thought

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Busy observing the sky in search of UFOs, Magoo accidentally finds himself wandering over to Fizz's house.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, hunting for Pinkbeard's treasure, mistakes the park for an island. Fizz and Weasel are there, selling ice cream that never falls on the ground.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo has decided to go mountain climbing but in reality, he is climbing up a skyscraper! He mixes Fizz up with a wounded bird and ruins his plan

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: A small injury means Mike needs to pay a visit to the vet, where he is made to wear the cone of shame while he gets better.

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The frogspawn in Bean's bathroom begin hatching, and before long the house is full of angry amphibians - just as the mayor is about to arrive for tea

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson travels to a ski resort for some fun.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Hoping to learn more about the zombie condition, Frankie invents a device which lets her swap bodies with Mum. But a power surge causes problems.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by guests Coronation Street star Beverley Callard, chefs Nick Nairn and Asma Khan, and food writers James and Paul Anderson, aka the Two Chubby Cubs, while wine expert Oz Clarke provides more helpful tips

programme.title: The Queen (2006)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning fact-based drama, examining the events surrounding the death of Princess Diana. The relationship between the monarch and Prime Minister Tony Blair is revealed as they struggle to reach a compromise in dealing with the situation. Starring Helen Mirren in an Oscar-winning role, alongside Michael Sheen, James Cromwell, Helen McCrory and Sylvia Syms

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 1.50 Denford Stakes, 2.25 Unibet You're On Handicap Stakes, 3.00 Irish Thoroughbred Marketing Geoffrey Freer Stakes and 3.35 Unibet Hungerford Stakes from Newbury, and three races from Newmarket - the 2.05 Heed Your Hunch at Betway Handicap Stakes, 2.40 Betway Handicap Stakes and the 3.15 Betway Grey Horse Handicap Stakes. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Luke Harvey, reporting from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Nick Cannon oversees the singing contest, as six mystery celebrities battle it out in while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as one-time Coronation Street actor Jimi Mistry (Khalid Nazir), former Emmerdale star Gemma Atkinson (Carly Hope), Loose Women's Nadia Sawalha and veteran actor James Cosmo pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for the charity of their choice. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the second episode, Stephen is joined by guests Joel Dommett, Chris Kamara, and Gemma Collins

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final day of this year's Battle Rounds in the junior version of the talent contest. Solo singers and duos between the ages of seven and 14 perform to impress judges Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am enough to make them spin their famous red chairs around. There are just six places left in the semi-final up for grabs, bringing the talented performers who win them one step closer to winning a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: For Your Eyes Only (1981)
programme.desc: James Bond goes in search of a British weapons system aboard a sunken vessel before it falls into the hands of enemies who will use it to deactivate all Western submarines. Unfortunately, his mission is hindered by a crossbow-toting woman out to avenge her father's murder. Spy thriller, starring Roger Moore, Topol, Julian Glover, Carole Bouquet, Jill Bennett and Lynn-Holly Johnson

programme.title: Captain Tom's War
programme.desc: Just a week after his 100th birthday celebrations, NHS fundraiser extraordinaire and national treasure Captain Tom Moore marks another upcoming milestone in this documentary - 75 years since VJ Day and the end of the Second World War later this summer. He looks back on his time in the brutal Burma campaign and honours the soldiers who fought in what has since become known - with so much focus on the European battlefront and on today's VE Day commemoration - as the 'forgotten war'

programme.title: Small Fortune
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Dermot O'Leary, in which teams of three take on challenges in miniature settings to win a cash prize of up to £150,000

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz, controlling a robot, distributes ice cream to everyone in the city. When Magoo mistakes him for a monster, he gets sprayed with ice cream.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is at the foot of a volcano, but in fact, he's next to a huge soup vat. And he sets it all awry.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz invents a cucumber that turns people into an object of worship. When Magoo sees people being drawn to Fizz and Weasel, he thinks it's zombies!

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A rogue AI seizes control of Thunderbird Five, trapping John on board. He tries to alert his brothers of the danger, but the program sends false images back to Tracy Island, leaving them oblivious to his plight

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is peeling off old wallpaper on Linda's walls, but he is actually destroying Fizz' hypnotic posters.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo finds himself shrunk and then dragged inside the President's head, along with Fizz. He thinks the hamster is a little, lost boy and decides to help him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is on a camping trip but he is actually just in the city park. He mistakes Weasel for a wounded bear and tries to help him.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is impressed with the other dogs jumping off a diving board, so he decides to try it himself.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Iris' hat is blown away by a strong wind and lands on the roof, so Mike decides it's time to be a hero.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean is invited to lunch at the stately home of an aristocratic lady who isn't all she seems. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben's jealousy has him convinced he has to out-do Kevin.

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games. This morning they are joined by Emmerdale actress Lisa Riley and Vamps singer Brad Simpson

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: The chef visits Northumberland, where he is joined by Michelin-starred chef Kenny Atkinson. The pair learn how Mead is made on Holy Island, and James cooks up a Singapore-inspired crab and lobster dish on a stunning beach, before they both enjoy a Puffin cruise to Cockett Island

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Juliet conjures up some ideas for Christmas, including baked party canapes, a brussels sprouts tarte tatin and a spectacular chocolate orange profiteroles tower. Liam Charles pays a visit to serve up Christmas lunch on a sheet of ruff puff pastry and Juliet visits chef Chantelle Nicholson in her London restaurant to sample a vegan main course Christmas meal

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three more factories producing some of the nation's favourite food-and-drink items. It reveals the secrets of traditional Lancashire Eccles cakes, using a recipe passed from father to son, jam fit for the Queen's breakfast table and gin infused with exotic natural ingredients at a hi-tech distillery. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher reaches Pembrokeshire, where he meets a family who have moved to the coast to live off-grid and takes part in an archeological dig to unearth the secrets of St Patrick's Chapel. He also joins members of Pembrokeshire Blind Society to find out how they use sound to experience the coast, and sees baby seals at Castlemartin military firing range

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final day of this year's Battle Rounds in the junior version of the talent contest. Solo singers and duos between the ages of seven and 14 perform to impress judges Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am enough to make them spin their famous red chairs around. There are just six places left in the semi-final up for grabs, bringing the talented performers who win them one step closer to winning a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Nick Cannon oversees the final episode of the singing contest, as the last six mystery celebrities battle it out in while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Octopussy (1983)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates the mysterious death of a British agent in East Berlin, a mission that leads him to a glamorous, international jewel thief. He discovers one of her criminal cohorts, an Indian gangster, is in league with a crazed Soviet general who plans to heat up the Cold War by detonating a nuclear bomb in an American Air Force base. Spy adventure, with Roger Moore, Maud Adams, Steven Berkoff and Louis Jourdan

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Julian Fellowes bids farewell to the Crawleys in the last small-screen episode, catching up with the Abbey's residents at the tail-end of 1925. Mary endeavours to build bridges, but Edith's secret continues to pose a threat. As Henry settles into the role of husband, scars left by Charlie's death are slow to heal, while Carson struggles to come to terms with developments that prove even he is not invulnerable to change. Spratt's moonlighting venture is threatened by the conniving of Denker, and Daisy may have missed her chance with an admirer. Isobel receives devastating news, while the day that Anna and Bates have long-since waited for arrives

programme.title: Bafta Celebrates Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross hosts a star-studded event at London's Richmond Theatre honouring the ITV drama, originally broadcast ahead of its final episode on Christmas Day 2015. There are reports from behind the scenes with cast and crew to provide an insight into one of the world‘s most popular shows, while creator Julian Fellowes takes to the stage to chat about its origins and secrets. Maggie Smith and Hugh Bonneville perform a specially written sketch, there are performances by Kiri Te Kanawa and Julian Ovenden, and Julie Walters presents the award to Fellowes and the cast and crew

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The first of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Oliver catches his teenage daughter Justina sneaking out of the house to attend a BLM march as he fears for her safety and is concerned she's putting herself at risk. Starring Nicholas Pinnock and Yasmin Monet Prince

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: A middle-class black woman is reunited with her working class white ex-boyfriend at a party. While waiting for a taxi, she talks over the past with him in a way they never had before, and reflect on the role racism and class have played in their relationship. Starring Adelayo Adedayo and Joe Cole

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The third of four short films addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Pippa Bennett-Warner and Paapa Essiedu star in Lynette Linton's drama, Look at Me, following the events that unfold when a couple are stopped by police on route to a restaurant, and how the incident impacts their lives

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The last of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter centres on an uncomfortable conversation between mixed-race Jordan, who has recently had a baby with a black man, and her mother Lyndsey. As things progress, they are forced to confront and evaluate their relationship as a seemingly immovable wedge appears between them. Written by and starring Nicole Lecky, with Amanda Abbington

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: The soap was founded on its dominating matriarchs and strong northern working-class woman and this edition celebrates the best of them - including the likes of Ena Sharples, Ivy Tilsley, Vera Duckworth, Blanche Hunt and Evelyn Plummer. Comedian Jason Manford narrates the programme that trawls the archives of the soap for its most memorable storylines, moments and characters from the past 60 years, with each episode carefully curated to bring alive each theme via specially selected highlights

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents action from the opening round of the campaign, which came from Donington Park

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.desc: Just a week after his 100th birthday celebrations, NHS fundraiser extraordinaire and national treasure Captain Tom Moore marks another upcoming milestone in this documentary - 75 years since VJ Day and the end of the Second World War later this summer. He looks back on his time in the brutal Burma campaign and honours the soldiers who fought in what has since become known - with so much focus on the European battlefront and on today's VE Day commemoration - as the 'forgotten war'

programme.desc: Ben's jealousy has him convinced he has to out-do Kevin.

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games. This morning they are joined by Emmerdale actress Lisa Riley and Vamps singer Brad Simpson

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: The chef visits Northumberland, where he is joined by Michelin-starred chef Kenny Atkinson. The pair learn how Mead is made on Holy Island, and James cooks up a Singapore-inspired crab and lobster dish on a stunning beach, before they both enjoy a Puffin cruise to Cockett Island

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Juliet conjures up some ideas for Christmas, including baked party canapes, a brussels sprouts tarte tatin and a spectacular chocolate orange profiteroles tower. Liam Charles pays a visit to serve up Christmas lunch on a sheet of ruff puff pastry and Juliet visits chef Chantelle Nicholson in her London restaurant to sample a vegan main course Christmas meal

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three more factories producing some of the nation's favourite food-and-drink items. It reveals the secrets of traditional Lancashire Eccles cakes, using a recipe passed from father to son, jam fit for the Queen's breakfast table and gin infused with exotic natural ingredients at a hi-tech distillery. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher reaches Pembrokeshire, where he meets a family who have moved to the coast to live off-grid and takes part in an archeological dig to unearth the secrets of St Patrick's Chapel. He also joins members of Pembrokeshire Blind Society to find out how they use sound to experience the coast, and sees baby seals at Castlemartin military firing range

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final day of this year's Battle Rounds in the junior version of the talent contest. Solo singers and duos between the ages of seven and 14 perform to impress judges Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am enough to make them spin their famous red chairs around. There are just six places left in the semi-final up for grabs, bringing the talented performers who win them one step closer to winning a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Nick Cannon oversees the final episode of the singing contest, as the last six mystery celebrities battle it out in while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Octopussy (1983)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates the mysterious death of a British agent in East Berlin, a mission that leads him to a glamorous, international jewel thief. He discovers one of her criminal cohorts, an Indian gangster, is in league with a crazed Soviet general who plans to heat up the Cold War by detonating a nuclear bomb in an American Air Force base. Spy adventure, with Roger Moore, Maud Adams, Steven Berkoff and Louis Jourdan

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Julian Fellowes bids farewell to the Crawleys in the last small-screen episode, catching up with the Abbey's residents at the tail-end of 1925. Mary endeavours to build bridges, but Edith's secret continues to pose a threat. As Henry settles into the role of husband, scars left by Charlie's death are slow to heal, while Carson struggles to come to terms with developments that prove even he is not invulnerable to change. Spratt's moonlighting venture is threatened by the conniving of Denker, and Daisy may have missed her chance with an admirer. Isobel receives devastating news, while the day that Anna and Bates have long-since waited for arrives

programme.title: Bafta Celebrates Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross hosts a star-studded event at London's Richmond Theatre honouring the ITV drama, originally broadcast ahead of its final episode on Christmas Day 2015. There are reports from behind the scenes with cast and crew to provide an insight into one of the world‘s most popular shows, while creator Julian Fellowes takes to the stage to chat about its origins and secrets. Maggie Smith and Hugh Bonneville perform a specially written sketch, there are performances by Kiri Te Kanawa and Julian Ovenden, and Julie Walters presents the award to Fellowes and the cast and crew

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The first of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Oliver catches his teenage daughter Justina sneaking out of the house to attend a BLM march as he fears for her safety and is concerned she's putting herself at risk. Starring Nicholas Pinnock and Yasmin Monet Prince

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: A middle-class black woman is reunited with her working class white ex-boyfriend at a party. While waiting for a taxi, she talks over the past with him in a way they never had before, and reflect on the role racism and class have played in their relationship. Starring Adelayo Adedayo and Joe Cole

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The third of four short films addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Pippa Bennett-Warner and Paapa Essiedu star in Lynette Linton's drama, Look at Me, following the events that unfold when a couple are stopped by police on route to a restaurant, and how the incident impacts their lives

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The last of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter centres on an uncomfortable conversation between mixed-race Jordan, who has recently had a baby with a black man, and her mother Lyndsey. As things progress, they are forced to confront and evaluate their relationship as a seemingly immovable wedge appears between them. Written by and starring Nicole Lecky, with Amanda Abbington

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: The soap was founded on its dominating matriarchs and strong northern working-class woman and this edition celebrates the best of them - including the likes of Ena Sharples, Ivy Tilsley, Vera Duckworth, Blanche Hunt and Evelyn Plummer. Comedian Jason Manford narrates the programme that trawls the archives of the soap for its most memorable storylines, moments and characters from the past 60 years, with each episode carefully curated to bring alive each theme via specially selected highlights

programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Bertie's circumstances change suddenly, leaving Edith uncertain whether to risk telling him the truth or keep her past a secret at all costs. Mary wonders if she can ever make peace with her sister as hostilities escalate, Spratt shows some hidden talents and Molesley's first day as a teacher does not go according to plan. Mrs Patmore's bed and breakfast attracts unwanted attention, leaving her in need of the Crawleys' help

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mrs Tishell decides she can no longer hide her love for Martin and, fuelled by a cocktail of drugs, takes drastic action. While James Henry is in her care, she boards a bus out of Portwenn and leaves a message for Dr Ellingham to 'meet them at the castle', forcing Martin, Louisa, Ruth and PC Penhale to mount a desperate search for the pair. Starring Martin Clunes, Selina Cadell and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is buzzing with excitement as the final preparations are made for the Doc and Louisa's big day. However, Martin is irritated by PC Penhale, who wrongly presumes he is best man - and to make matters worse, the bride is late. This isn't the first time the couple have tried to get married - will the villagers have to put the confetti back in the box again? Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star, with John Marquez, Eileen Atkins and Ian McNeice

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam tells Imran that he's convinced Gary murdered Rick. Alya confides in Imran that she will sell her share of Speed Daal to distance herself from Geoff. And Karen the social worker promises Toyah she will put the fostering wheels in motion once she's received the reference from Leanne

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah admits to Adam that if Gary did kill Rick then he did it to protect her family. Realising the stress of Oliver's condition is getting to Steve, she offers a shoulder to cry on. And when Geoff refuses to let Alya buy him out of Speed Daal, she takes great delight in dropping a bombshell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fears that Sarah has shared his darkest secrets, Maria asks David to witness her wedding instead of a sick Audrey, and a spiteful Geoff tells Sally that Tim will always choose him over her

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Winter and Barnaby investigate when Lower Pampling's star cricketer Leo Henderson is found dead after a match in the grounds, pummelled to death by cricket balls. Barnaby is surprised to see DS Jones undercover as one of Leo's team-mates, and his former colleague reveals he is working on suspected cases of match-fixing in the area, but things take an even more sinister turn when captain Fitz Theara is stabbed through the heart with a cricket stump

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The life of an eccentric and reclusive couple comes under police scrutiny when a social services investigator is killed, and Barnaby must unearth generations of family secrets and decipher astronomical charts to find the murderer. Meanwhile, the detective's wife Sarah (Fiona Dolman, The Royal Today) arrives in Midsomer as the new head of a local school. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Phyllida Law, Edward Fox and Neil Pearson

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. They question a man seen in the area on the night of the killing, who protests his innocence and points the finger at a known gangster he claims was also at the scene. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: High-court judge Rachel Callaghan (guest star Juliet Stevenson) is critically injured after being shot in the underground car park of her apartment. When it becomes clear a hitman was responsible, the team tries to discover who hired him. As evidence mounts against the accused, the judge's health deteriorates, and Castle is faced with a choice between friendship and respect for a person's right to die. Bill Paterson, Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster ventures onto the Oxford Canal, which meanders its way through the heart of the gentle English countryside. En route he visits Tooley's Boatyard - the oldest working such facility in Britain, gets to play a hero of his - Inspector Morse, and bumps into a group of floating thespians

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Little Birchmarch is besieged by traffic until Ida's novel approach makes the Highway's Chief act

programme.desc: Adam tells Imran that he's convinced Gary murdered Rick. Alya confides in Imran that she will sell her share of Speed Daal to distance herself from Geoff. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fears that Sarah has shared his darkest secrets, Maria asks David to witness her wedding instead of a sick Audrey, and a spiteful Geoff tells Sally that Tim will always choose him over her

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla has an intruder in her office, Jamie confesses to Belle, and Mandy asks for Lydia's help

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The penny finally drops for Leyla, Dawn makes a discovery, and Lydia has her suspicions

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is no going back for Dawn, Nate suggests he skip work to support Tracy, and Harriet feels more guilty than ever

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Divorced couple Marion Williams and James McDonnell come together to search for the child they were forced to give up for adoption in the face of overwhelming family opposition when they were teenagers. Elsewhere, Cathie Cutler-Evans searches for her birth mother, Adrienne Powell, who had to give her up after being unable to offer Cathie a stable home as an unmarried 19-year-old

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: The arrival of Miss Lambe's forbidden love causes Charlotte to question her opinion of Sidney. Clara witnesses a moment between Esther and Sir Edward that raises her suspicions about their relationship, with devastating consequences for Esther. Period drama, starring Rose Williams and Theo James

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: George refuses to apologise for his marriage to Amelia and his father sends him a letter, cutting him off from the family and denying him his inheritance. All the friends set sail for Belgium, where Becky makes use of every opportunity to advance in society, laying on the charm with the great and the good. Even George is captivated by Becky's new glamour, as Amelia notices with mounting dread. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel about an amoral woman who cunningly manoeuvres her way to the top of Georgian high society. Starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a teenager with severe learning difficulties is killed in a fire at his home, Brooks and Devlin's investigation leads them to the last person they thought could be responsible. Matters are further complicated when Steel and Phillips come up against brash defence lawyer Dominic Peck, whose win-at-all-costs attitude and outrageous ego threaten the fair outcome of the trial. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny's younger brother Steve pays a visit, and soon begins to threaten her relationship with Merton. Meanwhile, David sets his sights on joining a mod gang causing havoc in the village. Sarah Tansey, Thom Fell, Maureen O'Brien and Robert East star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: While Angie's career in commercials goes from strength to strength, Ida fears that her son's marriage is growing weaker. Will Ida be able to stop herself interfering?

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla has an intruder in her office, Jamie confesses to Belle, and Mandy asks for Lydia's help

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is certain Terry is up to no good, Alma tries to keep out of Stephen's way, and Rita and Mavis are unnerved as they realise Norris could be a murderer

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Stephen tells Alma he does not want her to keep avoiding him. Mavis feels awful for having thought Norris a murderer, and Derek is relieved when Angela phones to speak to Cole

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police are still searching Home Farm for clues about Kim. Frank is furious that the police still suspect him. Terry is bored with Viv talking about Kelly and Vic

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police find Kim's car in the water at the quarry with a woman's body inside, and Frank is called to identify it. Eric announces his engagement to Dee in the Woolpack

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy chases joyriders tearing through Aidensfield and accidentally runs down Don Dibley's son during the pursuit. The boy's father demands compensation, but soon realises his problems have just begun as his daughter reveals she is pregnant. Steve finds himself in trouble and David invents a novel way to rid the estate of moles. Rural drama, starring Mark Jordon, James Carlton and Ted Robbins

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's new neighbours are offended by Mike's latest sculpture. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is determined to listen to classical music, but someone keeps interrupting. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dee is sad that her family cannot attend the wedding. Biff and Linda enjoy a night away in a hotel for Linda's birthday. Zak, Barry and Lisa get drunk together in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles check the damage caused by the rocket explosion, Zak is upset by how much time Lisa is spending with Barry, and Frank helps DI Cooke with her investigations

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma feels Mike cares more for the business than her. Jack feels Terry is only visiting because of their inheritance money. Norris is reconciled with Angela

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera wants to invite Terry to stay permanently, and he is interested to learn Jack has shares in a racehorse. Don is furious to hear Josie has resigned

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida's birthday celebrations give her the blues, and even Wilfred's arrival doesn't make her any happier

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster ventures onto the Oxford Canal, which meanders its way through the heart of the gentle English countryside. En route he visits Tooley's Boatyard - the oldest working such facility in Britain, gets to play a hero of his - Inspector Morse, and bumps into a group of floating thespians

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Little Birchmarch is besieged by traffic until Ida's novel approach makes the Highway's Chief act

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam tells Imran that he's convinced Gary murdered Rick. Alya confides in Imran that she will sell her share of Speed Daal to distance herself from Geoff. And Karen the social worker promises Toyah she will put the fostering wheels in motion once she's received the reference from Leanne

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah admits to Adam that if Gary did kill Rick then he did it to protect her family. Realising the stress of Oliver's condition is getting to Steve, she offers a shoulder to cry on. And when Geoff refuses to let Alya buy him out of Speed Daal, she takes great delight in dropping a bombshell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fears that Sarah has shared his darkest secrets, Maria asks David to witness her wedding instead of a sick Audrey, and a spiteful Geoff tells Sally that Tim will always choose him over her

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla has an intruder in her office, Jamie confesses to Belle, and Mandy asks for Lydia's help

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The penny finally drops for Leyla, Dawn makes a discovery, and Lydia has her suspicions

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is no going back for Dawn, Nate suggests he skip work to support Tracy, and Harriet feels more guilty than ever

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Divorced couple Marion Williams and James McDonnell come together to search for the child they were forced to give up for adoption in the face of overwhelming family opposition when they were teenagers. Elsewhere, Cathie Cutler-Evans searches for her birth mother, Adrienne Powell, who had to give her up after being unable to offer Cathie a stable home as an unmarried 19-year-old

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: The arrival of Miss Lambe's forbidden love causes Charlotte to question her opinion of Sidney. Clara witnesses a moment between Esther and Sir Edward that raises her suspicions about their relationship, with devastating consequences for Esther. Period drama, starring Rose Williams and Theo James

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: George refuses to apologise for his marriage to Amelia and his father sends him a letter, cutting him off from the family and denying him his inheritance. All the friends set sail for Belgium, where Becky makes use of every opportunity to advance in society, laying on the charm with the great and the good. Even George is captivated by Becky's new glamour, as Amelia notices with mounting dread. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel about an amoral woman who cunningly manoeuvres her way to the top of Georgian high society. Starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a teenager with severe learning difficulties is killed in a fire at his home, Brooks and Devlin's investigation leads them to the last person they thought could be responsible. Matters are further complicated when Steel and Phillips come up against brash defence lawyer Dominic Peck, whose win-at-all-costs attitude and outrageous ego threaten the fair outcome of the trial. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny's younger brother Steve pays a visit, and soon begins to threaten her relationship with Merton. Meanwhile, David sets his sights on joining a mod gang causing havoc in the village. Sarah Tansey, Thom Fell, Maureen O'Brien and Robert East star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: While Angie's career in commercials goes from strength to strength, Ida fears that her son's marriage is growing weaker. Will Ida be able to stop herself interfering?

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Drug dealers kidnap a woman's child in a bid to deter her from testifying against them at a forthcoming - forcing Dempsey to turn to shady character the Bogeyman for help

programme.title: Derren Brown: 20 Years of Mind Control: Live

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
